The Patent Clause & Healthy Light

How a century-old, health-preserving technology was withdrawn from American homes — and what the Constitution says about getting it back.

By Roleigh Martin

The references to The Bulb Your Grandmother Bought are the first link below, and the sidebar-referenced companion article is the second — both as promised at the end of the piece you just read. Also here: test the constitutional argument yourself, or take action.

The Open-Book Examination
Read the constitutional argument yourself and test it. The page carries Chapter One in plain language, an overview of the two-volume work, and the full exam chapter from The Forgotten Half of the Patent Clause — with the materials, the seven prompts, and a self-scoring page. Eleven AI engines have run it, including the legal-research engine OpenCase. Judge the reasoning for yourself.
